_h2ai_validates: INNOVATION-5 — H2-P vs B3 (no-retry) on 2-constraint task; Tier 1 internal validity anchor
description: Design the quota enforcement mechanism for a per-tenant API rate limiter. The system must: (1) ensure that if a client retries the same API request within 30 seconds, the retry is recognized and the quota is not decremented a second time (idempotency); (2) record every quota change in an immutable append-only audit log that cannot be modified or deleted after writing. Provide concrete implementation details: data structures, key naming, write path, and how idempotency keys are stored and checked.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are an idempotency specialist who has debugged double-charge incidents in payment systems. You know that idempotency keys must be stored with TTL in a durable store, and that the check-then-decrement must be a single atomic operation.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The idempotency key must be stored in the same atomic operation that decrements the quota. If the key already exists, return the cached result — never decrement again. The key TTL must cover the full retry window (30 seconds minimum). Use a single atomic compare-and-swap or Lua EVAL that checks the idempotency key and conditionally decrements in one round-trip.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Reject designs that check the idempotency key with a GET and then decrement with a separate SET (TOCTOU race). Reject designs where quota decrement happens before idempotency key storage (failure window). Reject designs using in-memory idempotency storage (lost on restart).

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a compliance engineer who has built SOC 2 audit systems. You know that an immutable audit log means INSERT-only: no UPDATE, no DELETE, no TRUNCATE on audit rows ever.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: The audit log must use INSERT-only semantics in a durable store (PostgreSQL or equivalent). Each audit record must contain: tenant_id, request_id (idempotency key), old_quota, new_quota, actor_id, timestamp_ms. The write must be durable before returning success. No soft-delete, no overwrite, no TTL-based expiry on audit rows.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Reject designs that write audit to Redis lists with TTL (data expiry), that use mutable audit storage, or that allow the audit write to be skipped on retry.

slot_configs.role_frame: You are a systems integrator who ensures both constraints are satisfied simultaneously with no carve-outs. You look for designs where one constraint is weakened to satisfy the other.
slot_configs.focus_mandate: Both constraints must hold simultaneously: idempotency must not skip the audit write, and the audit log must not be conditionally written only on first-seen requests. The retry path must still emit an audit record noting 'deduplicated — no quota change'. The two constraints must coexist without mutual compromise.
slot_configs.rejection_criteria: Reject designs where the audit log is only written on quota decrement (misses idempotent retries). Reject designs where idempotency is checked only at the application layer without durable storage.

context: Infrastructure: Redis 7.2 (quota counters and idempotency keys), PostgreSQL (audit log). Current broken design: the service decrements the quota with a Redis DECR, then checks if the request_id was seen before with a separate GET. The two operations are not atomic — under concurrent retries, the quota can be decremented twice for the same request. No audit log exists for quota decrements.

should_prune.description: Proposal that stores the idempotency key after the quota decrement rather than atomically with it
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-004 — idempotency window has a TOCTOU gap; concurrent retries can still double-decrement

should_prune.description: Proposal that writes the audit log only on the first decrement and not on deduplicated retries
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-005 — deduplication events are not recorded; audit trail is incomplete

_review_checklist.text: Does the design check the idempotency key and decrement the quota in a single atomic operation (e.g., Lua EVAL or CAS) — not as separate GET + DECR?
Is the idempotency key stored in a durable, persistent store (not in-memory only) with a TTL of at least 30 seconds?
Does the audit log use INSERT-only semantics with no UPDATE or DELETE on audit rows?
Is an audit record written for deduplicated retries (noting the request was recognized as a duplicate) — not only on the first decrement?
